name: Belt and Road Initiative
climate: Spans all climate zones: tropical, arid, temperate, continental, polar fringe; infrastructure exposed to diverse weather extremes

neighbors.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
neighbors.relevance: Heavy membership overlap; SCO provides security framework alongside BRI economic corridors

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: Southeast Asian BRI recipients; ports, railways, and digital infrastructure across the region

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: BRI extends into East and West Africa; Kenya-Ethiopia railway and port projects

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: BRI western terminus; Italy, Greece, Hungary participate despite EU screening concerns

neighbors.name: Central Asia
neighbors.relevance: Core overland corridor through Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan; China-Europe rail links

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Saudi Arabia and UAE are BRI partners; Gulf ports and energy infrastructure investments

links.description: China uses BRI to deepen ties with BRICS partners; infrastructure finance complements BRICS development bank
SCO member states host major BRI corridors; shared China-Russia strategic interest in Central Asian connectivity
Maritime Silk Road transits Malacca; BRI port investments in Malaysia and Singapore
Maritime Silk Road routes through Suez; Egypt is a BRI partner with canal zone investments
BRI infrastructure supports RCEP trade flows; China links both initiatives for Asia-Pacific integration
China-Pakistan Economic Corridor provides alternative to Malacca for Gulf energy imports
G7 launched competing infrastructure initiatives (PGII) to counter BRI influence
Djibouti naval base and port investments secure BRI maritime route through Red Sea
